page: Amazon Web Services r3.large is a memory-optimized third-generation server instance. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor running at 2.5 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 15.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.5 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 32 GB NVMe SSD, while the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps. The instance runs on the Xen hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates that the r3.large sits in the lower performance tiers. Its single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is measured at 1306.89, placing it in the bottom 25%, while its multi-core CPU score of 1725.07 falls in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth tests show 13.3 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 11.51 GB/sec for large block write operations, both categorized in the bottom 10%. Geekbench scores further confirm this positioning, with a single-core score of 700.0 and a multi-core score of 813.0.

Given its hardware profile, the r3.large is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high computational throughput or modern instruction sets like AVX-512. Database operations on Passmark scored 711.93, and Redis SET operations reached 0.75 million operations per second, both in the bottom 10%. The instance is best suited for legacy memory-cached applications, small database setups, or lightweight web serving where memory capacity is prioritized over raw CPU performance and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services r3.large is a memory-optimized, third-generation server instance running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs (1 physical core, 2 threads) clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 15.0 GB of RAM and a 32 GB NVMe SSD. Designed for memory-heavy workloads, its performance benchmarks are generally modest, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores falling in the bottom 25% and 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with large block write speeds at 11.51 GB/sec. Lacking AVX-512 support and featuring a 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance is best suited for legacy memory-intensive applications, small database operations, or basic caching layers where memory capacity is more critical than high-speed computing or rapid network throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r3.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 15.0 GB of RAM, and a 32 GB NVMe SSD, designed for legacy memory-intensive workloads and basic caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r3.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 15.0 GB RAM, and a 32 GB NVMe SSD for legacy caching workloads.
tagline: A legacy memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated vCPUs, 15 GB of RAM, and local NVMe storage for basic caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
15.0 GB of system memory (7.5 GB per core)
32 GB local NVMe SSD storage included
Xen hypervisor virtualization on x86_64 architecture
Suited for legacy memory-cached applications and small databases
